text: The subject property consists of three units?Units A, B, and C?which have undergone various interior and exterior updates in recent years, as reported by the property owner. Unit A, the west-facing two-story unit, received a bathroom update in 2018, including tile flooring, a marble vanity, and fresh paint. In 2023, the bedroom was improved with a new ceiling, lighting, paint, resurfaced flooring, and custom wall panels. A former utility room was converted into a large closet. That same year, the kitchen was updated with painted cabinets, new hardware, replaced flooring, ceiling treatments, recessed lighting, a new stove and refrigerator, and a new backsplash. The entire unit was repainted with new trim paint in 2023, and a laundry room was added in 2020. Unit B had a bathroom remodel and laundry hookups added in 2018, along with two window replacements completed in 2023. Unit C underwent a complete attic remodel in 2021, including renovations to the bathroom and living space. Updates consisted of new windows, drywall, fixtures, flooring, lighting, doors, and electric heating. The kitchen was also updated with new cabinets, flooring, a raised ceiling, lighting, stove, microwave range, refrigerator, and a pantry with shelving. A laundry room with new washer and dryer units was added. Exterior improvements include furnace replacements for all three units within the past five years, with air conditioning units also replaced in Units A and C during that time. A new roof was recently completed, the front porch was renovated, and the driveway was replaced. A visual inspection of the subject's basement was performed at the time of the appraisal. No visible areas of immediate repair were noted; however, some dampness was observed along the north side of the basement. This condition appears to be the result of significant rainfall occurring within 24 hours prior to the inspection. The appraiser does not hold the necessary qualifications to be considered an expert in foundation condition or restoration. All information regarding the property's condition and renovations was supplied by the owner and observed during the property inspection. All utilities were on and appeared to be working properly during the inspection.

All information regarding the property's condition and renovations was supplied by the owner and observed during the property inspection. All utilities were on and appeared to be working properly during the inspection.   Underwriter Conditions  Revision: 1.) Can the appraiser please comment if the opening to the stairs to finished attic is a safety hazard? If so, please consider updating the report subject to installation of railings.  - Although a handrail is currently installed on the attic staircase, the open stairwell presents a potential safety hazard. To mitigate this risk, additional railings should be installed at the stairwell opening. As a result, the appraisal has been revised from 'as-is' to 'subject to' the installation of these safety railings.

Continued from Condition of the Property: components have been rehabilitated/re-manufactured into like-new condition. Recently constructed improvements that have not been previously occupied are not considered "new" if they have any significant physical depreciation (i.e., newly constructed dwellings that have been vacant for an extended period of time without adequate maintenance or upkeep). The use of a hypothetical condition might affect assignment results.
